typeSystem_what.abstract: The E.L.I.A. language employs an extended orthogonal type classification model, based not on a single criterion but on a set of independent dimensions.

typeSystem_why.abstract: The E.L.I.A. language is built around a paradigm that places permissibility of operations at its core (permissibility-first).

typeSystem_how.abstract: E.L.I.A. associates every data element with a 64-bit Type Metadata envelope  that enables deterministic parsing, validation, and serialization...

seq.Note: A record is a pure data construct

record_what.abstract: A record  represents a structured block-materialized, schema-defined derived value composed of named fields.

record_why.abstract: The philosophical separation between Record and Class is enforced in E.L.I.A.

record_how.abstract: A record declaration MUST introduce a schema-defined derived type with a stable semantic identity (TYPE_HASH).

class_what.abstract: A Class is a *capable behavioral construct* that represents a concrete, state-bearing execution unit.

class_why.abstract: A class represents a concrete role realization within a domain. It is an internal behavioral carrier that fulfills one or more contracts but...

interface_what.abstract: An interface represents a role declaration within a domain. It defines a set of required operations, expected inputs/outputs, and semantic...

interface_how.abstract: An interface MUST be declared using the canonical define interface form.

seq.Note: Error as a type (data, not behavior)

error_what.abstract: represent an immutable failure fact suitable for deterministic classification and evidence preservation;

error_how.abstract: intrinsic property MUST be initialized without the property keyword

event_what.abstract: represent an immutable factual occurrence within a business or system context;

event_how.abstract: An event declaration MUST introduce a schema-defined derived type with a stable semantic identity (TYPE_HASH).

event_when.abstract: E.L.I.A. distinguishes two classes of inter-domain event propagation:

flow_what.abstract: A flow represents a process-level execution plan within a domain. It defines *when* and *under which conditions* actions are executed, and *which...

flow_how.abstract: Admission logic MUST reside exclusively in flows.

action_what.abstract: An action represents a named business function within a domain. It declares an operation that is meaningful at the business level and can be...

action_how.abstract: (a) An action MAY be invoked only from within a flow.

seq.Note: Semantic Normative Class (ANNEX D → ANNEX E)

rule_what.abstract: A rule is a pure decision specification that evaluates semantic conditions and produces a truth value, classification, or decision outcome. Rules are classified into normative classes R0–R6 with strict purity, dependency, and evaluation-stage constraints.

rule_why.abstract: All semantic reasoning in E.L.I.A. is required to be:

seq.Note: Rule normative classes R0–R6 catalog (ANNEX D)
Dependency and purity constraints matrix (ANNEX D)
Rule chaining, closure, and deterministic sequencing (ANNEX D)

rule_how.abstract: A rule MUST NOT act as an execution substrate. Rules are classified into 7 normative classes (R0–R6) with strict dependency purity, evaluation-stage, and chaining constraints.

seq.Note: Evaluation stages: compile/resolve/bind, ingress/admission, runtime, boundary
Rule classes R0–R6 with evaluation-stage assignments
Record compatibility and stage-constraint rules (ANNEX D)

rule_when.abstract: Rules are evaluated at four distinct stages: compile/resolve/bind time (R2), ingress/admission time (R3), boundary time (R1/R4), and runtime (R0/R5/R6). Stage determines allowed inputs and surfaces.

binder_what.abstract: A binder is a pure transformation specification used to bridge structural mismatches, normalize external payloads, and adapt values at...

binder_how.abstract: A binder MAY be applied only at explicit boundaries using an explicit using <Binder> modifier.

inline_what.abstract: inline is a directive for compact declarative specification of standardized operations inside an action body.

inline_how.abstract: reduce verbose imperative code for standardized operations;

inline_when.abstract: reduce verbose imperative code for standardized operations;

definition_what.abstract: Definition is a first-class semantic construct that *designates meaning and obligations* for a domain concept.

definition_why.abstract: A definition in E.L.I.A. is not documentation. It is a semantic boundary.

seq.Note: Constraint levels: constraint, must, should, may

explanation_what.abstract: Explanation is a first-class semantic construct responsible for making *meaning explicit, inspectable, and traceable* for both humans and machines.

explanation_how.abstract: The presence and attachment of Explanation content is governed by the following normative rules:

domain_what.abstract: binds abstract meaning to real-world responsibility;

domain_why.abstract: A domain in E.L.I.A. is not a service and not an infrastructure unit. A domain is a semantic authority.

domain_how.abstract: Every semantic construct MUST belong to exactly one Domain.

delegate identity, classification, and semantic role
what does delegate construct represent and define
delegate_what.abstract: Delegate is a first-class semantic construct that establishes an explicit trust and execution boundary between E.L.I.A.

target: how to declare a delegate with syntax and binding
delegate declaration pipeline, bind to and mapped to
how delegate retry and circuit breaker work
delegate_how.abstract: A Delegate declaration defines a typed external interaction contract and MAY additionally define an external binding target and a deterministic...

delegate_when.abstract: E.L.I.A. produces both preventive signals (build-time) and authoritative enforcement (runtime).

dataInterface_what.abstract: A Data Interface is a first-class semantic construct that projects defined meaning onto concrete data surfaces.

seq.Note: General rules, binder roles, binding classes

dataInterface_how.abstract: A Data Interface MUST declare exactly one RecordType and one KeyType

semanticInterface_what.abstract: A Semantic Interface is a first-class semantic construct that *projects defined meaning into admissible behavioral interaction*.

semanticInterface_why.abstract: E.L.I.A. does not introduce a single “contract” keyword. Instead, a contract emerges from the semantic composition of constructs:

seq.Note: Conflict resolution: supersede, combine, retire, deprecated

surrogate_what.abstract: A Data Surrogate is a first-class data artefact that becomes *semantically visible* by attaching Explanation mechanics (and optionally...

seq.Note: Surrogates concern — why they exist in enforcement model
Data surrogate enforcement attachment — why implicit attachment matters
Behavioral surrogate enforcement — why explicit attachment matters

surrogate_why.abstract: Surrogates preserve semantic integrity and enforcement continuity during evolution. any erases semantics (preserving security), tuple erases security (preserving semantics) — both are controlled degradation points in the enforcement model.

surrogate_how.abstract: A Data Surrogate MUST be one of the following:

target: what is enforcement architecture for delegate trust boundaries
what is enforcement graph and compliance pipeline
what is delegate trust boundary enforcement
enforcement_what.abstract: The architectural enforcement and compliance model of E.L.I.A. as an executable, traceable, and auditable pipeline.

target: why enforcement exists for delegate trust boundaries
why trust boundary enforcement and compliance by construction matter
why delegate boundaries need enforcement architecture
enforcement_why.abstract: E.L.I.A. exists to address a structural failure common to modern systems:

target: how enforcement pipeline enforces delegate trust boundary step by step
how delegate trust boundary is enforced via admission control
how cross-border enforcement controls delegate boundary crossings
enforcement_how.abstract: The enforcement pipeline describes how meaning becomes admissible execution and how violations surface as observable facts.

enforcement_when.abstract: Admission control defines whether an execution is eligible to enter the domain’s execution graph.

seq.Note: Semantic layer in the language model

semanticModel_what.abstract: Semantic model in E.L.I.A. is declarative and deterministic (consists of finite set of primitives).

semanticModel_why.abstract: The internal logic of E.L.I.A. is built upon the same deep information concepts that underlie modern Large Language Models, but applies them in a...

seq.Note: Inline resolution: toolchain view, resolved outcome

semanticModel_how.abstract: Defines *strategic semantic intent*. It establishes priority, value ordering, and interpretative frames under which multiple meanings are allowed to coexist.

domainAuthority_what.abstract: Domain Semantic Authority is layered. Semantic meaning emerges through explicit language constructs that differ by Layer.

domainAuthority_how.abstract: Layer 1 defines the semantic space in which domains may exist. It establishes the *rules of the world*, not the authority of a specific domain.

persistence_how.abstract: Persistence MAY occur only where a persist instruction is explicitly declared inside a flow.

stream_what.abstract: Stream types represent sequential, potentially unbounded data carriers designed for controlled input/output, transport, and incremental...

stream_how.abstract: A stream is a semantic I/O boundary, not a transport primitive.

dfd_how.abstract: Layer 1 defines intent, prohibitions, and obligations.

antiPatterns_what.abstract: The following patterns are explicitly prohibited, as they violate Domain Semantic Authority, semantic inspectability, and responsibility boundaries.

seq.Note: Consequences: ambiguity, audit loss, inability to reason

antiPatterns_why.abstract: Violation of the semantic hierarchy invariants defined above produces structural damage to the language model, not merely local defects.

syntaxConstraints_how.abstract: define domain "<Name>" with version, effective_date, deprecated_date.

resilience_what.abstract: Data and behavior serve fundamentally different roles in a resilient system and are therefore subject to different rules, guarantees, and failure models.

resilience_how.abstract: Admission logic MUST reside exclusively in flows.
